applications.next_action: Prepare system-design answer around data validation and model-adjacent workflow boundaries.
applications.notes: Demo record showing interview preparation and generated artifacts.

texts.evaluation: Strong fit for systems-first ML/data engineering. Main risk: avoid presenting model training depth as production experience. Position around pipelines, validation, documentation and operational constraints.
texts.cover_letter:
Dear hiring team,

I am interested in the ML/Data Systems Engineer role because it sits at the boundary I work best in: data flows, automation, validation, and practical AI tooling. My strongest contribution is not to oversell model work, but to make model-adjacent systems inspectable, documented, and safe to operate.

Best,
Dídac
texts.interview_guide: Prepare: data contracts, validation layers, failure surfacing, monitoring, and stakeholder translation. Ask where pipeline failures are usually detected first: source data, model behavior, or downstream operations.

call_card.call_signals: Data quality, reproducible workflows, model-adjacent engineering, stakeholder translation.
call_card.technical_reading: Likely needs someone who can move between data pipelines, validation, and applied ML constraints without overselling model authorship.
call_card.pitch: Systems-first profile: data flow, tooling, validation, documentation, and domain accountability.
call_card.smart_question: Where do pipeline failures usually surface first: data contracts, model behavior, or downstream operations?
call_card.risk_to_avoid: Do not frame conceptual ML knowledge as production model ownership.
call_card.prepare_first: Data contracts, validation checks, and examples of turning messy inputs into controlled artifacts.
call_card.prepare_later: Monitoring, governance, and how generated artifacts are reviewed before public use.

offer.raw_text: Demo offer excerpt: build and maintain data workflows, collaborate with ML teams, validate data quality, document operational assumptions, and support internal AI tooling.
offer.cleaned_text: ML/data systems role focused on data workflows, validation, documentation, and AI tooling support.

research.summary: Demo company focused on operational analytics and internal AI tooling.
research.business_model: B2B analytics platform and implementation services.
research.red_flags: Clarify whether ML work means model development, data engineering, or prompt/tooling support.

form_answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
form_answers.draft_answer: Because the role connects data workflows, validation, documentation, and AI tooling, which is where my current work is strongest.

applications.next_action: Send follow-up with concise project evidence and boundary-oriented workflow examples.

texts.evaluation: High positioning value. Main evidence should be repo-backed workflow design, review gates, static/public boundary control, and human approval loops.
texts.cover_letter: Demo follow-up: I can show a concrete workflow where public pages, local data, validation, and generated artifacts are separated instead of blurred into one unsafe automation surface.
texts.interview_guide: Prepare examples: local/private tracker boundary, static site generation, PR review loop, and why agent outputs need receipts.

call_card.call_signals: Agent orchestration, review gates, public/private data boundaries, workflow observability.
call_card.technical_reading: Role likely needs practical workflow design rather than abstract prompt enthusiasm.
call_card.pitch: I design agentic systems where state, evidence, generated artifacts and human approval are kept inspectable.
call_card.smart_question: Which failures matter most in your workflows: wrong context, unsafe writes, stale state, or unreviewed generated output?
call_card.risk_to_avoid: Avoid sounding like the work is just prompt writing.
call_card.prepare_first: Explain file-based state, validation, PR checks and public/private separation.
call_card.prepare_later: Discuss how to test workflow contracts without freezing temporary implementation details.

applications.next_action: Wait for recruiter response; prepare backend/domain-rule examples.

call_card.call_signals: Backend systems, domain rules, tooling, documentation, maintainability.
call_card.technical_reading: Position around clear domain transitions and operational constraints.
call_card.pitch: Backend work is strongest when rules, permissions, failures and data transitions are explicit.
call_card.smart_question: Which domain transitions currently create the most support or maintenance cost?
call_card.risk_to_avoid: Do not over-center AI if the role is backend-first.
call_card.prepare_first: Spring/Java project explanation and domain-rule examples.

applications.next_action: Decide whether the AI/product angle matches current campaign direction.

texts.evaluation: Potentially attractive but requires careful positioning around product engineering and workflow tooling, not generic AI enthusiasm.

Data pipelines.short_definition: Structured movement and transformation of data into reliable downstream artifacts.
Data pipelines.detail: For interviews, explain where validation, monitoring and ownership enter the flow.
Data pipelines.interview_angle: Ask where failures are detected first and who owns corrections.

Validation.short_definition: Checks that data, outputs, or generated artifacts satisfy explicit constraints before use.
Validation.detail: Validation matters because generated or transformed outputs can look plausible while being operationally wrong.
Validation.interview_angle: Discuss schema checks, review gates, tests and manual approval points.

Model behavior.short_definition: Observed behavior of an ML or AI model under real inputs, constraints and failure cases.
Model behavior.detail: Useful framing when you support model-adjacent systems without claiming ownership of model training.
Model behavior.interview_angle: Separate conceptual ML knowledge from production model ownership.

Generated artifacts.short_definition: Outputs created by a workflow, such as cover letters, form answers, PDFs or interview guides.
Generated artifacts.detail: The important part is traceability: generated artifacts need source context, review and storage boundaries.
Generated artifacts.interview_angle: Explain how artifacts are reviewed before public or external use.

Governance.short_definition: Rules for accountability, data handling, approvals, traceability and operational limits.
Governance.detail: For this project, governance means private data stays local and public pages remain curated summaries.
Governance.interview_angle: Ask how approvals and responsibility are handled around generated outputs.

Agentic workflows.short_definition: Workflows where model calls, deterministic tools, review gates and human decisions are explicitly separated.
Agentic workflows.detail: A useful agentic workflow is inspectable and recoverable, not just an uncontrolled chain of prompts.
Agentic workflows.interview_angle: Discuss state, evidence, boundaries and failure recovery.

Context boundaries.short_definition: Explicit limits around what an agent, module or workflow may see, infer, modify or expose.
Context boundaries.detail: Boundaries avoid leaking private context into public outputs or giving an agent more authority than needed.
Context boundaries.interview_angle: Explain public/private split and why raw intake is local-only.

Review gates.short_definition: Human or automated checkpoints before changes become public, committed or externally sent.
Review gates.detail: Review gates prevent temporary drafts from becoming public claims or stable architecture by accident.
Review gates.interview_angle: Connect this to PRs, CI and manual approval.

Static export.short_definition: A generated static artifact that can be hosted without a backend.
Static export.detail: For the tracker demo, static export means fixed public demo data rendered through the same dashboard code path.
Static export.interview_angle: Use it to explain deployed/private boundary management.

Backend.short_definition: Server-side logic, persistence, APIs, domain rules and operational behavior.
Backend.detail: Backend positioning should focus on behavior and constraints rather than framework-name dropping.
Backend.interview_angle: Ask about domain transitions, support costs and failure modes.

Domain rules.short_definition: The business constraints that define valid state changes and behavior.
Domain rules.detail: Domain rules are where backend work becomes more than CRUD.
Domain rules.interview_angle: Discuss examples around permissions, state transitions and validation.

Testing.short_definition: Automated or manual checks that verify behavior against expectations.
Testing.detail: Tests should protect real contracts, not freeze temporary implementation mistakes.
Testing.interview_angle: Mention the difference between useful contract tests and brittle over-specification.

Documentation.short_definition: Technical explanation that preserves intent, constraints and operational knowledge.
Documentation.detail: Documentation is part of system reliability when agents and humans collaborate over time.
Documentation.interview_angle: Explain how docs prevent stale context and future misinterpretation.

Product engineering.short_definition: Engineering work that balances user value, system constraints and implementation tradeoffs.
Product engineering.detail: Position carefully: product sense plus technical execution, not vague product enthusiasm.
Product engineering.interview_angle: Ask how product decisions are validated technically.

Workflow orchestration.short_definition: Coordinating tasks, data movement, tools, dependencies and execution order.
Workflow orchestration.detail: In agentic systems, orchestration must include boundaries and review, not only sequencing.
Workflow orchestration.interview_angle: Discuss how failures are surfaced and resumed.

AI tooling.short_definition: Tools that make AI-assisted work usable, inspectable and integrated into real workflows.
AI tooling.detail: The tooling layer often matters more than the model call itself.
AI tooling.interview_angle: Explain review surfaces, generated artifacts and limits.
